howItWorks.intro: Every state requires hunters and anglers to purchase licenses before heading afield. Fees fund conservation and wildlife management programs.
rules: Resident vs non-resident: non-resident licenses typically cost 3–10x more than resident licenses
Hunting license: required base license for all game hunting in the state
Species tags: deer, elk, turkey, and other big game require separate tags on top of the base license
Waterfowl: requires a state waterfowl stamp plus a federal duck stamp ($25)
Combo licenses: hunting + fishing bundles often save 10–20% vs buying separately
Most licenses are valid for one year from date of purchase or for the calendar/fiscal year
howItWorks.outro: Prices shown are base state fees. Additional habitat stamps, access fees, or HIP registration may be required. Always check your state wildlife agency for current pricing before purchasing.

faq.question: How much does a hunting license cost?
faq.answer: Resident hunting licenses typically cost $10-$50 depending on the state. Non-resident licenses are significantly more expensive, ranging from $75 to over $300. Additional tags for specific species are extra.

faq.question: What is the federal duck stamp?
faq.answer: The Federal Migratory Bird Hunting and Conservation Stamp (duck stamp) costs $25 and is required for all waterfowl hunters aged 16 and older. Revenue goes directly to wetland habitat acquisition. You need this in addition to your state waterfowl stamp.

faq.question: Do I need a separate tag for deer?
faq.answer: Yes. In most states, you need a base hunting license plus a deer tag or permit. Some states include one deer tag with the license, but many charge separately. Antlerless tags, muzzleloader tags, and archery tags may be additional.

faq.question: How do I establish residency for hunting licenses?
faq.answer: Most states require 6-12 months of continuous residency, a state driver's license, and/or voter registration. Military personnel stationed in a state often qualify for resident rates. Residency fraud is a serious offense in every state.

faq.question: Can I buy a license online?
faq.answer: Most states now offer online license purchasing through their wildlife agency website. Some charge a small convenience fee ($1-$5). You can usually print a temporary license immediately and receive the physical card by mail.

faq.question: Do children need a hunting license?
faq.answer: Requirements vary by state. Many states offer free or reduced licenses for youth under 16. Some states require hunter education certification for all first-time buyers regardless of age. Most require adult supervision for minors.
